title: Nephrolepis lunulata
intro: Nephrolepis lunulata, commonly known as the fishbone fern, is a type of epiphytic fern native to tropical regions. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in wound healing, reducing inflammation, and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Nephrolepis lunulata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin problems.
taxonomy: Nephrolepis lunulata is a species of fern in the family Nephrolepidaceae. It belongs to the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. This plant is native to tropical regions and is commonly known as the fan fern or sword fern. The genus Nephrolepis is part of the larger kingdom Plantae. Nephrolepis lunulata is also known for its distinctive fan-shaped fronds.
uses: Nephrolepis lunulata, also known as the fishbone fern, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant's extracts have been studied for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to protect against various health issues. In some cultures, Nephrolepis lunulata is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves are also believed to have wound-healing properties, making it a potential natural remedy for minor cuts and scrapes. More research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Nephrolepis lunulata for medicinal purposes.

parts: Nephrolepis lunulata, commonly known as the silver vase fern, does not have widely recognized or documented medicinal parts.
